message:
You ask your dog a question, and there it is — that adorable little head tilt. 🐶 But it turns out it's about much more than just being cute.

Researchers believe dogs tilt their heads to see and hear us better. A dog's muzzle can actually block part of their view of your face, so a little tilt helps them read your expression. It may also help them pinpoint exactly where a sound is coming from. And here's the sweetest part: studies of especially clever dogs found they often tilt their heads right before remembering the name of a favorite toy — a tiny sign of deep concentration. 🧠

So the next time your pup tilts their head at you, take it as a compliment — they're really, truly paying attention. 💛

Does your dog tilt their head at certain words? Tell me which ones below! 🐾
